# getbased

> Free, open-source blood work dashboard that runs entirely in your browser.
> Import any lab PDF with AI, track biomarker trends over time, and get
> AI-powered interpretations. No account required. Your data never leaves
> your device.

## What getbased does

- Import any blood work PDF or image — AI extracts all markers automatically (any format, any language, batch import)
- Track trends over time with interactive charts across 16+ biomarker categories (287+ markers)
- Specialty labs supported: OAT (organic acids), fatty acids (Spadia, ZinZino, OmegaQuant) via custom marker pipeline
- DNA import — upload raw data from 23andMe, Ancestry, MyHeritage, FTDNA, or Living DNA. 42 curated SNPs across 10 health categories with APOE haplotype resolution
- Biological age — PhenoAge (Levine 2018) + Bortz Age combined, with chronological age reference line
- AI chat panel with debate mode — pit two AI personas against each other over your results. Conversation threads, image attachments, web search hints
- DNA-aware supplement and lifestyle recommendations
- 9 lifestyle context cards (diet, exercise, sleep, stress, etc.) shape AI interpretation
- Menstrual cycle tracking with phase-aware reference ranges, perimenopause detection, iron alerts
- Baubiologie EMF assessment — room-by-room measurements graded against SBM-2015 standards
- Correlation viewer, heatmaps, compare dates, trend alerts, PDF reports
- E2E encrypted cross-device sync via Evolu CRDT — self-host the relay for full control
- OpenClaw messenger bot integration — chat about your labs over Telegram, Signal, or any messenger
- Fully local-first — all data stored in browser localStorage, encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM), auto-backup
- 4 AI providers: OpenRouter (200+ models, recommended), Anthropic, Venice (with optional E2E encryption), or Local AI (Ollama, LM Studio, Jan)

## Key pages

- [Landing page](https://getbased.health/): Feature overview and getting started
- [Web app](https://app.getbased.health): Launch the dashboard (no install needed)
- [Documentation](https://app.getbased.health/docs): User guides, setup, and feature walkthroughs
- [Blog](https://getbased.health/blog): Development stories and health data insights
- [GitHub](https://github.com/elkimek/get-based): Source code (GPLv3 license)
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/zJdVB9zgQB): Community and support

## Docs highlights

- [Getting started](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/getting-started): First import walkthrough
- [AI setup guide](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/ai-chat): Configure OpenRouter, Anthropic, Venice, or Ollama
- [DNA import](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/dna-import): Upload raw DNA data and view genetic insights
- [Cross-device sync](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/cross-device-sync): E2E encrypted sync setup and self-hosting
- [OpenClaw](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/openclaw): Connect your messenger bot
- [Privacy and security](https://app.getbased.health/docs/guide/encryption): Local-first architecture, encryption, PII obfuscation

## How getbased differs from alternatives

- **vs InsideTracker / SelfDecode**: getbased is free, open-source, and runs entirely in your browser. No subscription, no data collection, no account required
- **vs spreadsheets**: AI-powered PDF import extracts markers automatically. Interactive charts with reference/optimal bands, trend alerts, and correlation analysis
- **vs generic health trackers**: getbased is built specifically for lab results. 287+ biomarkers across 16+ categories, specialty labs (OAT, fatty acids), DNA import (42 SNPs), biological age, menstrual cycle phase-aware ranges, and 9 lifestyle context cards that shape every AI interpretation
- **vs ChatGPT/Claude directly**: getbased structures your data, maintains history across imports, and gives AI your full health context — not just a single lab report pasted into a chat window. Connect via OpenClaw to chat over any messenger
